message:
feat(mvcc): add version-chain length controls with eager GC and backpressure

Add per-page version-chain length enforcement to the MVCC transaction
manager, preventing unbounded chain growth that degrades read latency on
hot pages. When a page chain exceeds the configured max_chain_length
(default 64), eager GC prunes versions below the minimum active snapshot
horizon. If GC cannot reduce the chain within busy_timeout_ms, the commit
returns MvccError::Busy (backpressure).

Key changes across the MVCC crate:

- ebr.rs: Add 5 new EBR metrics (max_chain_length_observed,
  chain_length_samples_total, chain_length_sum_total, gc_freed_count,
  gc_blocked_count) with recording helpers and snapshot avg_chain_length().
  Replace CAS loop in record_guard_pinned with atomic fetch_max.

- invariants.rs: Add chain_length() for observing committed chain length
  of a single page (retries on GC race), and prune_page_chain_eager()
  for caller-driven GC at an explicit horizon.

- lifecycle.rs: Introduce max_chain_length/chain_length_warning config,
  active_snapshot_highs tracking with cached_gc_horizon, and
  enforce_chain_bound_for_page() called during publish_write_set().
  publish_write_set() now returns Result to propagate Busy errors.
  Register/unregister active snapshots on begin/read/commit/abort.

- physical_merge.rs: Fix cell sort ordering to handle mixed rowid/index
  cells correctly with explicit match arms instead of if-let.

- ssi_validation.rs: Sort incoming edges deterministically (by txn id,
  epoch, then overlap page) to match outgoing edge determinism for
  reproducible proof generation.

- witness_publication.rs: Replace 3 remaining .expect("lock poisoned")
  calls with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) for mutex poison
  resilience.

- lib.rs: Re-export EbrMetrics, EbrMetricsSnapshot, GLOBAL_EBR_METRICS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
